programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Leeds United (Kick-off 7.56pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, as the 13-time winners host the Championship promotion contenders at the Emirates Stadium. These sides last met at the same stage of this competition eight years ago, with Gunners legend Thierry Henry scoring the only goal of the game. The Frenchman - Arsenal's all-time leading scorer - was making his second debut for the club, having joined on a short-term loan from New York Red Bulls. Leeds boss Marcelo Bielsa will be hoping his team can cause an upset this time around, as the home side have flattered to deceive in the Premier League so far this campaign(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having started a fight with a stranger, for the crime of having a conversation with his girlfriend, Liam thinks back to his adolescence and where this toxic behaviour stems from. Comedy written by and starring Liam Williams, which straddles two timelines to explore the roots of modern-day masculinity by delving into the memories of his own misspent adolescence(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Brand New Tour
programme.desc: A stand-up performance filmed as part of Kevin's 2018 UK and Ireland tour and his 19-night residency at the Glasgow Hydro. The comic gives his take on the modern world, social media, anxiety, religion, conversations with his dog and having Barack Obama as his support act(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville present stories of tourists who were left stranded by airlines - including a look at how insurance is not always a protection(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Goodinson Family samples life in New Zealand for a week to decide if they want to make a permanent move - but health concerns mean time is not on their side(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A medical student tracks down the hit-and-run driver who has left him for dead, a thief is tempted by a slab of fudge, and a wildlife camera catches burglars in the act(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Thomas Plant help two teams in Builth Wells, Powys, and presenter Natasha Raskin finds out about the history of Cardiff Bay with local expert James Cowen(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Emma try to hatch a plan to free Jimmi, but Rob delivers a warning. Sid treats a prison officer who has been advised by Jimmi on the inside(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah catches up with homeowners in Suffolk and Cambridgeshire, discovering how they have transformed their new properties and made new lives for themselves(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray is frustrated when Chantelle cannot give him the news he wants - and later, to make matters worse, he is unimpressed to see his wife talking to Jags in the Square. Ben has some harsh words for Louise when she berates him for talking about Sharon. Whitney is shocked to hear Leo is opening a stall on the market, tensions rise between Karen and Billy, and Jean is unimpressed when Mo suggests the Slaters should hold a seance to speak to Daniel(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is discharged from the psychiatric unit, having made progress with her recovery - so is able to help a troubled Kian as he battles demons of his own. Max encounters a face from the past and is blackmailed into performing a dangerous surgery - if she refuses, her most painful secrets will be revealed to the world(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: Five vegans head to Merthyr Tydfil to try to convert local meat-eaters to a plant-based lifestyle, challenging views on both sides of the debate in the process. They set up a welcome event in the town centre, but while some of the group try to win the locals over with cake, another favours the hard-hitting approach of playing footage from abattoirs in the town centre(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke tackle a neglected and boring backyard in Birmingham for its owner Sophie, who wants the area full of birds, bees and butterflies(n)

programme.title: The Robe (1953)
programme.desc: A callous Roman centurion is assigned to oversee Christ's crucifixion, an experience he finds so moving that he decides to convert to the Christian faith in a bid to redeem himself. However, his new loyalties incur the wrath of the insane Emperor Caligula. Drama based on the novel by Lloyd C Douglas, starring Richard Burton, Jean Simmons, Victor Mature and Michael Rennie(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown is in Somerset celebrating the work that volunteers and communities do in our countryside. He spends the day on the community farm that's bringing people together to learn about food, helping with mental health issues and doing its bit for the environment and sustainability. Steve also helps with a bee count, and meets the man who has turned his life around through woodworking(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from Saturday Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary discovers the rich history of our nation's greatest stately homes through its food, beginning with Highclere Castle, the real-life Downton Abbey, renowned as the setting for some of the most lavish and influential weekend house parties in history. She takes to the estate kitchen, where she creates a delicious spread including raspberry tartlets and gamekeeper's stew, and an elaborate menu for a grand finale dinner(n)

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Village
programme.desc: Documentary following the wildlife near a remote French village, including red squirrels, wild boar, dormice and stone martens. As the animals struggle to survive the winter, they take full advantage of living so close to unsuspecting humans in their search for food, while trying not to be detected. Narrated by Tcheky Karyo(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Part two of two. The horticultural enthusiast explores Japan during autumn. He begins as he did in the spring, at one of the 'three great gardens of Japan' to learn how they are protected from the coming winter. Monty follows the history of the Japanese garden through the military strongholds of past leaders to the many styles, new and old, of the iconic stroll garden and finds a slice of Victorian England in the heart of Tokyo. He also learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle. He ends his journey by the Sea of Japan at a place that makes art out of its gardens(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents the show from Crowcombe Court, west Somerset, where experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant discover some promotional Victorian sausage plates(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes Baroness Tanni Grey-Thompson on a journey to Somerset to recreate a childhood holiday of 1980. The pair travel in a bright yellow Rover, just like her family car back in the day, and they visit the Cameley Lakes where the former Paralympian would have stayed. They also visit Longleat Safari park, go fishing, and visit the Wells Cathedral(n)

programme.title: Tomorrowland: A World Beyond (2015)
programme.desc: A boy genius in the 1960s finds his way into a spectacular futuristic metropolis while attempting to promote his idea for a jetpack. Decades later, he has grown into a disillusioned adult, while a girl who has the talent and enthusiasm he has lost also finds her way into this strange new world, and together they attempt to uncover its secrets. Fantasy adventure, starring George Clooney, Britt Robertson and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: The long-running show returns with action from the slalom event in Zagreb, Croatia. Plus, interviews with Mikaela American star Shiffrin and double amputee snowboarder Darren Swift, and a look at whether Dave Ryding can secure Great Britain's first-ever World Cup victory(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell assess the profit-making potential of three more business ideas - a range of boxing technology, a coffee business that may be on shaky ground financially, and a female fashion advisor offering a new look to male clients. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Geraint Thomas: The Road Will Decide
programme.desc: Documentary following the 2018 Tour de France winner as he battled to retain the title. The film captures the intensity of his journey as he negotiates 21 stages and 3,500km in 23 days, while facing extreme weather conditions, cancelled stages and competitors such as France's Julian Alaphilippe(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian tests his preconceptions of Albania, guided on a whistlestop tour of the country by local TV presenter and journalist Erjona Rusi. Along the way, he tries to discover why Albania has remained a stubbornly unfashionable holiday destination, meeting those at the extremes of the country, from the rural farmers to the billionaire playboys who live in the cities in the company of supermodels(n)

programme.title: Dara O Briain - Voice of Reason
programme.desc: An exclusive chance to see the comedian Dara O Briain's Voice of Reason live show from 2019, filmed at London's Eventim Apollo. In the show, Dara addresses problems with virtual reality, having too much technology at home, how to deal with missing members of the front row and how to cope with reports of your own demise(n)

programme.title: Mustang (2015)
programme.desc: Five sisters lead a carefree life in northern Turkey, until accusations from neighbours place them at the centre of a scandal. Their family responds by forcing them to stay at home and pressuring them into arranged marriages - but the girls band together to reassert their independence. Drama, starring Gunes Sensoy and Doga Zeynep Doguslu. In Turkish(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, the American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena finds her principles tested when she encounters the dealer who supplied drugs to her dead daughter, and is forced to make a life or death decision. Chloe finally feels ready to move forward in her life, but may have to destroy the hopes of a good friend to do so. Ange and Fletch prepare for their first New Year's Eve together, but Sacha and Dominic's interference could ruin their plans. Guest starring Charlie Condou (formerly Coronation Street's Marcus Dent)(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having visited Australia in a camper van 20 years ago, Emma Howells wants to move her family Down Under. However, husband Gerald is less sure. A trial week gives them the chance to see what life could be like for them in Perth, but will it give the couple answers or drive them further apart? Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The six strongest chefs from the heats compete in the quarter-final, inventing a dish with onion as the key ingredient. After the weakest two are sent home, the others cook two courses for three critics - William Sitwell, Tracey MacLeod and Amol Rajan. However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in the next round(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: In an edition devoted to Britain's harbours, Nicholas Crane visits Newlyn, Cornwall, where in 1854, a fishing boat set sail on the 12,000-mile journey to Melbourne, Australia. Dick Strawbridge explores the building of airships in Barrow-in-Furness, Cumbria, in the early 1900s, while Tessa Dunlop travels to Portsmouth to investigate the history of body art. Ruth Goodman hears how Birkenhead's harbour led to the opening of the world's first municipal park and there is a look at naval warfare re-enactments by Scarborough council staff(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Cameras follow friends and families as they work out how to fund and find a rental property abroad to escape the worst of the British weather. Dave and Trish want to leave the winter in Sussex behind and rent a piece of paradise in the Algarve, with local property agent Nick on hand to help them find what they're looking for in Portugal. But will they take the plunge and make the move south for winter? Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of economic recipes, including a Bake Off masterclass featuring Sussex suet pond pudding, and a James Martin tomato soup with garlic bread(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Frances de la Tour looks into her family, starting out with a tantalising clue that hints she may have aristocratic ancestors. Delving into the history of her three-times great-grandmother, she uncovers the story of a secret lover, an illegitimate child and a landmark divorce case. But the scandal does not stop there and she goes back a further generation, where sure enough she uncovers a tragic story leading her to a historic family residence(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Raj Bisram value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, and Paul Martin finds out how the myth of Merlin has been connected to the heart of Wales(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with a winner declared each Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with an interwar Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region(n)

programme.title: The Twinstitute
programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken put health theories to the test with the help of pairs of identical twins, revealing ways we can live a healthier life. Thanks to their matching DNA, identical twins are the perfect candidates for scientific comparison, so Chris and Xand use this principle to put contrasting health theories to the test. In the first episode, the doctors pit diet against exercise, and analyse the results as they challenge twins to go head-to-head with contrasting regimes to see who loses the most weight(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on specialist subjects of the Gordon Riots of 1780, the history of Marvel comics, Franklin D Roosevelt and FE McWilliam, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as two teams compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one question asking what links Small cut, Beautiful Country, Sweet milk, and Grain from the Po Valley(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the quiz for students continues, with two teams representing their universities aiming to reach the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Aylesbury Prison
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone undertakes a challenge to form a choir in HMYOI Aylesbury, a prison for young offenders serving sentences from four years to life. First he meets Governor Laura Sapwell, who feels that a choir might be a different and unique way to engage with the young men as part of a programme aimed at trying to prevent people reoffending. At the end of his first week, Gareth has met almost all the prison's 200 residents, with 20 having expressed real interest in working with him(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer look back at the year the Chippendales had their first British tour, Haagen-Dazs advertising took off and DJ Sasha was part of the wave of celebrity DJs. Nirvana's classic Nevermind album hit the shops and jump-started the grunge sound, and The Commitments proved the Irish had soul(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Shadow Commander: Iran's Military Mastermind
programme.desc: For decades Iranian commander General Qassem Soleimani, who was killed in a US drone strike last week, dominated the brutal struggle for power and influence across the Middle East. During the Iraq War he is said to have smuggled thousands of sophisticated bombs into the country, arming the Shia militias who killed hundreds of British and American troops. But just a few years later the western coalition found themselves on the same side as Soleimani as they both fought to defeat the Islamic State. This documentary includes first-hand accounts of his secret deals and shifting alliances across Syria, Iraq and Lebanon(n)

programme.title: Drinkers Like Me: Adrian Chiles
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the reasons he drinks alcohol as he attempts to find out why he and many others do not think they are addicted to it, despite finding it almost impossible to enjoy life without it. Adrian - who drinks almost every day - starts a diary and soon finds out his intake is way over the recommended limit, so decides to get his liver checked out and the results come as a shock(n)

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: From his early days in comedy troupe The Jockeys of Norfolk, to his breakthrough role as Charles in Four Weddings and a Funeral, Hugh Grant has become one of Britain's best-loved stars, with hits including two Bridget Jones movies, Notting Hill, Love Actually, and Paddington 2. More recently, he has turned his talents to television in the acclaimed drama A Very English Scandal, playing notorious politician Jeremy Thorpe. Aside from the Bafta-winning actor discussing his career, friends and colleagues including Andie MacDowell, Richard Curtis, Sandra Bullock, Colin Firth, Ang Lee, Stephen Frears and Nicholas Hoult also add their thoughts(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: This week, the candidates travel to the world-famous BBC Maida Vale Studios in West London, where Lord Sugar informs them that for their latest challenge, they are to become managers for musicians. The teams must audition unsigned artists before choosing which one to represent. They will then head to the studio to create a brand-new remix of one of their tracks, aiming to secure sales of the record at their own showcase events. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown is in Somerset celebrating the work that volunteers and communities do in our countryside. He spends the day on the community farm that's bringing people together to learn about food, helping with mental health issues and doing its bit for the environment and sustainability. Steve also helps with a bee count, and meets the man who has turned his life around through woodworking(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to prepare a croque monsieur in 15 minutes for Marcus Wareing, while Monica Galetti sets three more the task of making pea risotto. The six contestants move on to the Signature Dish stage, where they must inspire both judges with their own unique creation before three are chosen to go through to the quarter final and three leave the contest(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nicholas Crane tells the tale of the Great Storm of 1703, which saw ships blown from British shores to Norway, while Ian McMillan discusses the fate of hospital ship Rohilla, which ran aground off Whitby, North Yorkshire, in 1914. Dick Strawbridge visits Tiree in the Inner Hebrides, where he explains how the timings of the D-Day landings were determined by RAF weather forecasters(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: With the aid of a property expert, a Leeds duo hope to rent their house so they can spend winter in the Costa Blanca. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes to sate cravings for a takeaway, including fish and chips from James Martin and a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Charles Dance made his name playing aristocrats - a far cry from his own background, having been born to a parlour maid. He wants to know if he comes from a long line of servants or if he can uncover some grander origins. The actor is also determined to find out about his father, who died when he was four and about whom he knows hardly anything - a search that takes him to the other side of the world(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum at Southsea in Hampshire, antiques expert Charles Hanson uncovers a navigational tool and Catherine Southon enthuses about a decorative Japanese cabinet dating back to the 1870s. Plus, Paul Martin finds out what it was like to serve as Royal Marine commando(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. The opening episode introduces the first set of the six expectant families featured in the series, including young first-time parents Syler and Mo Nicholson-Bayoumi from Nottinghamshire, and Helen and Nigel Pierce, who are expecting their fourth child(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Documentary about the Cornish fishing village of Mevagissey. While many fishing communities are struggling, this one has thrived owing to protection from prevailing winds and returning fish stocks. Boats have been handed down through generations and currently 74 operate out of the harbour. As young captains prepare to follow in their father's footsteps, tourism also becomes a major boost to the town - but leaves it feeling deserted in winter. A veteran skipper prepares to retire, and questions what the next generation will learn from his example(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Aylesbury Prison
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gareth desperately needs to expand his choir, with only seven inmates committing to the project and just three weeks before they have to put on a concert. He finds fresh hope in an encounter with a young man who has turned the story of his descent into crime into a song - and his example inspires others to share their own personal experiences. However, Gareth still needs voices, so tries to recruit some of the prison officers to help(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam helps Max settle in to her first days of college life in Chicago and has an unsettling return journey back to Los Angeles(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look back at the wild card play-offs(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Snow Queen
programme.desc: A performance by Scottish Ballet inspired by Hans Christian Andersen's fairytale and celebrating the company's 50th anniversary, recorded at Edinburgh's Festival Theatre(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate more potentially valuable works of art, beginning by helping a man who believes he owns a painting by French post-impressionist Edouard Vuillard. The quest for provenance begins in Geneva, where Philip compares the picture with a huge Vuillard work created in 1919, trying to find out whether the same materials were used in both, while Fiona uncovers clues to the picture's origins in France and Holland. Once they have enough evidence, they seek the approval of the Wildenstein Institute in Paris - but these are the same experts who notoriously rejected a highly credible Monet back in the very first episode. Have they done enough to prove this picture is genuine?(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Fara Dabhoiwala trawls five decades of the BBC archive to explore how documentary-makers have shifted their attitude toward explorers over the past five decades(n)

programme.title: Lucy Worsley: Elizabeth I's Battle for God's Music
programme.desc: The presenter investigates the story of choral evensong, which was instigated by Henry VIII during the tumultuous and violent era of the English Reformation. Lucy recounts how Elizabeth I and her two siblings were shaped by the changes their father brought about and how her ideas about religious music found their ultimate expression(n)

programme.title: The Many Primes of Muriel Spark
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents a celebration of the author of The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie on the 100th anniversary of her birth. She revisits Spark's Edinburgh roots, talks to biographer Alan Taylor and conducts the first televised interview with Penelope Jardine, who was a close friend of the writer for 40 years. With contributions from AL Kennedy, Janice Galloway, Ali Smith, William Boyd and Val McDermid(n)

programme.title: Rebel Women: The Great Art Fightback
programme.desc: The story of the revolutionary feminist artists inspired by the cultural revolution of the 1960s, paying tribute to those that paved the way and examining their legacies. Featuring interviews with pioneers Judy Chicago, Margaret Harrison and Mary Kelly, as well as Lubaina Himid, Suzanne Lacy, Carolee Schneemann, Rose English, Laurie Simmons and Barbara Kruger(n)

programme.title: Darcey Bussell: Looking for Margot
programme.desc: Margot Fonteyn inspired generations of ballerinas, while her late-flowering partnership with Rudolf Nureyev created the most dazzling ballet partnership in history. However, her life was marked by tragedy and disappointment. Darcey finds that Margot couldn't find love and never had children, got drawn into a failed foreign revolution, danced on for too long, and died in poverty. The former ballerina goes behind the scenes at the Royal Opera House and the Royal Ballet, and travels from London to New York and Panama, discovering that Margot did eventually find a kind of happiness - though one that came at a high price(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: In a small workshop in a country house in Wiltshire, silversmith Simon Benney makes distinctive jewellery and household objects for the royal family and private clients. Simon is following in the footsteps of his father, Gerald, who revolutionised the design of British silverware in the post-war era. This film follows the making of an engraved, gold and diamond pendant by Simon, featuring a trademark enamel finish using techniques his father learnt from Faberge(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques(n)

programme.title: Eat to Live Forever with Giles Coren
programme.desc: The food critic takes up three extreme regimens in an effort to push the limits of life expectancy and meets people from around the world whose pursuit of longevity is an obsession that dominates every aspect of their lives. He undertakes the calorie restriction diet, before going on one that consists only of foods hunted, fished or gathered by mankind's Paleolithic ancestors and ends with another made up almost entirely of fruit. Throughout the process, Giles's health is monitored by his doctor, who assesses the impact these dietary habits are having on his body(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in today's culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors(n)

programme.title: The Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his exploration of the artistic history of the northern European region by looking at Sweden's tradition of furniture design(n)

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores the development of the art of photography in the UK, beginning by looking at how science and technology allowed pioneering photographers like Roger Fenton and Julia Margaret Cameron to create a new art form in the 1800s(n)

programme.title: Hokusai: Old Man Crazy to Paint
programme.desc: Biography of the world-renowned Japanese artist Katsushika Hokusai, whose print The Great Wave off Kanagawa helped make his name. With Andy Serkis reading the voice of Hokusai, the film features artists David Hockney and Maggi Hambling, and scholars who study, admire and venerate this Japanese master(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: In a small workshop in a country house in Wiltshire, silversmith Simon Benney makes distinctive jewellery and household objects for the royal family and private clients. Simon is following in the footsteps of his father, Gerald, who revolutionised the design of British silverware in the post-war era. This film follows the making of an engraved, gold and diamond pendant by Simon, featuring a trademark enamel finish using techniques his father learnt from Faberge(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer travels around the islands and mainland coast of the Caribbean Sea, beginning on Hispaniola. He joins the Dominican Republic police's anti-narcotics division, before crossing the border to Haiti to see the notorious tented slums of Cite Soleil, while also discovering a vibrant, colourful and thriving side to the country. He ends this leg of his journey in Puerto Rico, an archipelago that is a territory of the United States, and examines the legacy of bombing and weapons testing on the island of Vieques(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of 1970s cult leader Jim Jones. The series explores how he was forced to flee the US, establishing a settlement in a remote part of Guyana, and the violent end of the project in 1978. After cult members opened fire on a US delegation, killing five people, Jones persuaded 918 of his followers to join him in mass suicide - including 304 children(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: The historian celebrates British homes, starting with a visit to the picturesque Warwickshire countryside and the village of Stoneleigh, which has barely changed in 500 years, its cottages perfectly preserved. Here he charts the cottage's transformation from humble hovel to modern dream home(n)

programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A revisit to an impressive project at large Grade II listed building in Rochester, kent. The team also explore properties in Mountain Ash, South Wales, and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetah cubs have never been born at the park before, so the staff keep a close eye on first-time mum Wilma, as she copes with the new arrivals(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant offer their expert assistance to two teams scouring Kingston upon Thames seeking keenly priced items to take to auction in an effort to maximise their profits against each other. Host Charlie Ross observes their progress and also finds a piece of vintage tea advertising that might well be worth a packet(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: Return of the programme in which Kate and her Welsh sheepdog Teg explore Wales' countryside. She begins in North Wales, trying to climb the tallest man-made structure in the country to fix some loose cables on the Arfon Transmission Tower. Next, Kate learns how to overcome natural obstacles with the novel activity of packrafting - using a lightweight inflatable canoe. On the north-east coast, Kate meets a pack of dogs trained to carry out wildlife conservation by sniffing out endangered species, then on the north-west coast she digs and prepares a grave for a new resident who in death wants to give himself to help nature thrive(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Bristol to explore how Methodist Church founder John Wesley devised a service that still helps many Christians mark the new year(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who are journalists find a home in Devon with holiday let potential on a budget of £700,000. Away from the hunt, he meets a young sheep farmer(n)

programme.title: The 1900 Island
programme.desc: Four families with a longing to escape the demands of the modern world experience life in a fishing community as it was at the turn of the 20th century, heading to the small island of Llanddwyn off the coast of Anglesey. In the opening programme, the Powers, the Davies, the Barkers and couple Kate Evans and Arwel John all come together and realise they must adapt quickly - but bad weather, lack of experience and limited rations lead to hunger and frustrations. Can a last-minute effort by the women save the day?(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Liverpool v Everton (Kick-off 4.01pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the third-round clash between the Merseyside rivals, which takes place at Anfield. These sides met on this ground in the Premier League last month, with the Reds prevailing 5-2 in what proved to be Marco Silva's final game in charge of the Toffees. The teams last played each other in the FA Cup at this same stage two years ago, and Jurgen Klopp's men also emerged victorious on that occasion, albeit by a closer 2-1 scoreline. Defender Virgil van Dijk scored the winner on his debut, following a £75million move from Southampton a matter of days beforehand(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The return of the period drama about midwives and nurses in London's East End, picking up the story in January 1965. After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Dr Stephen Ward reports the suspicious theft of some of his private photographs, and persistent rumours force Minister of War John Profumo into more denials and threats of legal action. Mandy Rice-Davies is enjoying her newfound press exposure, but the sordid tales of London's society sex parties and affairs with young models embarrass Christine's family. With Johnny's trial approaching, she considers running away from her problems(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield, Sheffield United v AFC Fylde at Bramall Lane and Middlesbrough v Tottenham Hotspur at Riverside Stadium. This was the second Merseyside derby at this stage in the last three seasons, while non-league Fylde aimed to produce one of the competition's great shocks against Chris Wilder's Premier League outfit, and Spurs boss Jose Mourinho began his bid to win the trophy he claimed with Chelsea in 2007(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning with the story of an NHS nurse facing $350,000 in hospital fees after her holiday insurers refused to pay her American medical bills(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Glasgow to look round a cottage flat, as well as another cottage in Gnosall, Staffordshire. Dion Dublin is in Teddington, London to appraise a one-bed flat(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A blind busker has his day's takings stolen, a hotel receptionist puts an armed robber in his place and a helpful neighbour gives police a live commentary on a break-in(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at the helm of this show from Nottingham racecourse. Steph Connell and Thomas Forrester help their teams and Anita gets to the bottom of a local UFO(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi's new cellmate makes life difficult for him, while Ruhma helps a new mother cope with her baby's two overbearing grandmothers. Medical drama set in a busy Midlands surgery(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to rural Shropshire to meet one couple who had £425,000 and wasted no time in snapping up one of the houses they viewed on their 'Escape' property hunt(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which antiques experts search the UK for items to sell as they compete to make the most money at auction. Catherine Southon and James Braxton start off in the Home Counties(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Leeds United (Kick-off 7.56pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, as the 13-time winners host the Championship promotion contenders at the Emirates Stadium. These sides last met at the same stage of this competition eight years ago, with Gunners legend Thierry Henry scoring the only goal of the game. The Frenchman - Arsenal's all-time leading scorer - was making his second debut for the club, having joined on a short-term loan from New York Red Bulls. Leeds boss Marcelo Bielsa will be hoping his team can cause an upset this time around, as the home side have flattered to deceive in the Premier League so far this campaign(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having started a fight with a stranger, for the crime of having a conversation with his girlfriend, Liam thinks back to his adolescence and where this toxic behaviour stems from. Comedy written by and starring Liam Williams, which straddles two timelines to explore the roots of modern-day masculinity by delving into the memories of his own misspent adolescence(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Brand New Tour
programme.desc: A stand-up performance filmed as part of Kevin's 2018 UK and Ireland tour and his 19-night residency at the Glasgow Hydro. The comic gives his take on the modern world, social media, anxiety, religion, conversations with his dog and having Barack Obama as his support act(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville present stories of tourists who were left stranded by airlines - including a look at how insurance is not always a protection(n)

programme.desc: The Goodinson Family samples life in New Zealand for a week to decide if they want to make a permanent move - but health concerns mean time is not on their side(n)

programme.desc: A medical student tracks down the hit-and-run driver who has left him for dead, a thief is tempted by a slab of fudge, and a wildlife camera catches burglars in the act(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.desc: Al and Emma try to hatch a plan to free Jimmi, but Rob delivers a warning. Sid treats a prison officer who has been advised by Jimmi on the inside(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show(n)

programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray is frustrated when Chantelle cannot give him the news he wants - and later, to make matters worse, he is unimpressed to see his wife talking to Jags in the Square. Ben has some harsh words for Louise when she berates him for talking about Sharon. Whitney is shocked to hear Leo is opening a stall on the market, tensions rise between Karen and Billy, and Jean is unimpressed when Mo suggests the Slaters should hold a seance to speak to Daniel(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is discharged from the psychiatric unit, having made progress with her recovery - so is able to help a troubled Kian as he battles demons of his own. Max encounters a face from the past and is blackmailed into performing a dangerous surgery - if she refuses, her most painful secrets will be revealed to the world(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce from The Call Centre explores the issues that are affecting her generation, beginning by attempting to go sober for four weeks. Like many people her age, 29-year-old Hayley Pearce wouldn't dream of a night out without alcohol, but with recent studies revealing that nearly a third of 16-24 year olds now don't drink at all, Hayley wants to understand what's behind the growing tee-total trend among young people. To find out, she attempts to go sober for four weeks(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce tests out her maternal side as she explores what it means to be a 'mam' for two weeks, undertaking midwife training and looking after a simulation baby(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment(n)

Whitney is shocked to hear Leo is opening a stall on the market, tensions rise between Karen and Billy, and Jean is unimpressed when Mo suggests the Slaters should hold a seance to speak to Daniel(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Bob is torn between fixing his family and taking care of business. AJ tries to make up for what he has missed, and Stevie and Poppy work out what they mean to each other(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is discharged from the psychiatric unit, having made progress with her recovery - so is able to help a troubled Kian as he battles demons of his own. Max encounters a face from the past and is blackmailed into performing a dangerous surgery - if she refuses, her most painful secrets will be revealed to the world(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: Five vegans head to Merthyr Tydfil to try to convert local meat-eaters to a plant-based lifestyle, challenging views on both sides of the debate in the process. They set up a welcome event in the town centre, but while some of the group try to win the locals over with cake, another favours the hard-hitting approach of playing footage from abattoirs in the town centre(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the third-round fixtures, including Manchester City v Port Vale at the Etihad Stadium and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester United at Molineux. Holders City hadn't faced the Valiants since October 1999, and were heavy favourites to progress against their League Two opponents. Wolves overcame United at the quarter-final stage last season, winning 2-1 on this ground thanks to second-half goals from Raul Jiminez and Diogo Jota. Plus, highlights of Rochdale or Boston United v Newcastle United, Bristol City v Shrewsbury Town, Millwall v Newport County, Rotherham United v Hull City, Burnley v Peterborough United, Birmingham City v Blackburn Rovers, Fulham v Aston Villa, Cardiff City v Forest Green Rovers or Carlisle United, Oxford United v Exeter City or Hartlepool United, Southampton v Huddersfield Town, Brighton & Hove Albion v Sheffield Wednesday, Bristol Rovers or Plymouth Argyle v Coventry City or Ipswich Town, Reading v Blackpool, Watford v Tranmere(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A revisit to an impressive project at large Grade II listed building in Rochester, kent. The team also explore properties in Mountain Ash, South Wales, and Birmingham(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lindy MacEwan thinks New Zealand could give her the fresh start she wants, but husband Steve has reservations, so they take their children down under for a trial week(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin and Thomas Plant offer their expert assistance to two teams scouring Kingston upon Thames seeking keenly priced items to take to auction in an effort to maximise their profits against each other. Host Charlie Ross observes their progress and also finds a piece of vintage tea advertising that might well be worth a packet(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley is in Bristol to explore how Methodist Church founder John Wesley devised a service that still helps many Christians mark the new year(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple who are journalists find a home in Devon with holiday let potential on a budget of £700,000. Away from the hunt, he meets a young sheep farmer(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Liverpool v Everton (Kick-off 4.01pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the third-round clash between the Merseyside rivals, which takes place at Anfield. These sides met on this ground in the Premier League last month, with the Reds prevailing 5-2 in what proved to be Marco Silva's final game in charge of the Toffees. The teams last played each other in the FA Cup at this same stage two years ago, and Jurgen Klopp's men also emerged victorious on that occasion, albeit by a closer 2-1 scoreline. Defender Virgil van Dijk scored the winner on his debut, following a £75million move from Southampton a matter of days beforehand(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: A compilation programme championing cottage industries, with Ellie Harrison meeting a Herefordshire woman who sells hand-crafted products all revolving around fire(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. A terrifying plan to destroy humanity is about to reach fruition. Can the Doctor and her companions escape multiple traps and defeat a deadly alliance? Jodie Whittaker, Tosin Cole, Bradley Walsh and Mandip Gill star(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The return of the period drama about midwives and nurses in London's East End, picking up the story in January 1965. After dealing with a power cut at the maternity home, Fred follows an unusual sound and finds a newborn baby abandoned in a dustbin. Meanwhile, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane must deal with an alarming outbreak of diphtheria, and Sister Julienne receives news of an ambitious redevelopment programme that potentially threatens Nonnatus House. Starring Jenny Agutter and Stephen McGann(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Dr Stephen Ward reports the suspicious theft of some of his private photographs, and persistent rumours force Minister of War John Profumo into more denials and threats of legal action. Mandy Rice-Davies is enjoying her newfound press exposure, but the sordid tales of London's society sex parties and affairs with young models embarrass Christine's family. With Johnny's trial approaching, she considers running away from her problems(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Liverpool v Everton at Anfield, Sheffield United v AFC Fylde at Bramall Lane and Middlesbrough v Tottenham Hotspur at Riverside Stadium. This was the second Merseyside derby at this stage in the last three seasons, while non-league Fylde aimed to produce one of the competition's great shocks against Chris Wilder's Premier League outfit, and Spurs boss Jose Mourinho began his bid to win the trophy he claimed with Chelsea in 2007(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Kelly Somers presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City at The Hive Stadium(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts a special edition celebrating 50 years of the quiz. She and regular team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson take a trip down memory lane as they are joined by three distinguished past guests in four-time Major-winning golfer Laura Davies, the most prolific try-scorer in English rugby league history Martin Offiah, and triple world champion gymnast Beth Tweddle, as well as former Rangers and Scotland striker Ally McCoist, who was once a team captain on the show(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning with the story of an NHS nurse facing $350,000 in hospital fees after her holiday insurers refused to pay her American medical bills(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits Glasgow to look round a cottage flat, as well as another cottage in Gnosall, Staffordshire. Dion Dublin is in Teddington, London to appraise a one-bed flat(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A blind busker has his day's takings stolen, a hotel receptionist puts an armed robber in his place and a helpful neighbour gives police a live commentary on a break-in(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is at the helm of this show from Nottingham racecourse. Steph Connell and Thomas Forrester help their teams and Anita gets to the bottom of a local UFO(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi's new cellmate makes life difficult for him, while Ruhma helps a new mother cope with her baby's two overbearing grandmothers. Medical drama set in a busy Midlands surgery(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to rural Shropshire to meet one couple who had £425,000 and wasted no time in snapping up one of the houses they viewed on their 'Escape' property hunt(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The return of the programme celebrating inspirational farming families and the rural events where they showcase their hard work. Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which antiques experts search the UK for items to sell as they compete to make the most money at auction. Catherine Southon and James Braxton start off in the Home Counties(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Leeds United (Kick-off 7.56pm). Coverage of the third-round fixture, as the 13-time winners host the Championship promotion contenders at the Emirates Stadium. These sides last met at the same stage of this competition eight years ago, with Gunners legend Thierry Henry scoring the only goal of the game. The Frenchman - Arsenal's all-time leading scorer - was making his second debut for the club, having joined on a short-term loan from New York Red Bulls. Leeds boss Marcelo Bielsa will be hoping his team can cause an upset this time around, as the home side have flattered to deceive in the Premier League so far this campaign(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: A portrait of caravan life on the Ards Peninsula, telling the stories of those who leave their problems behind in the city and head to the seaside. In a revealing depiction of working-class Protestant life, this documentary scratches beneath the stereotype of the affectionately nicknamed Shankill by the Sea to understand what really draws people to holiday here(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: A profile of Chloe Ho, who is Northern Ireland's only female Chinese lion dancer, which she sees as a way to express her culture, overcome shyness and have a sense of belonging(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having started a fight with a stranger, for the crime of having a conversation with his girlfriend, Liam thinks back to his adolescence and where this toxic behaviour stems from. Comedy written by and starring Liam Williams, which straddles two timelines to explore the roots of modern-day masculinity by delving into the memories of his own misspent adolescence(n)

programme.title: Graham Norton's Good Show Business Guide
programme.desc: Sometimes the best thing in a talk show is the business in-between. Here, some of the world's biggest stars prove they can do so much more than chat when they are on the famous red sofa. Examples include Tom Cruise directing a dangerous action sequence with members of the audience, Margot Robbie demonstrating her tattooing skills, Chris Pratt showing off his magic tricks, Morgan Freeman reprising his most famous role, Taylor Swift facing the massed ranks of her Twitter fans, Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson delivering his well-known catchphrase, Anthony Joshua challenging Greg Davies to a re-match, and Olivia Colman showcasing her majestic voice(n)

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Brand New Tour
programme.desc: A stand-up performance filmed as part of Kevin's 2018 UK and Ireland tour and his 19-night residency at the Glasgow Hydro. The comic gives his take on the modern world, social media, anxiety, religion, conversations with his dog and having Barack Obama as his support act(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. In addition - with more Commons archive - Bercow Bows Out offers the chance to re-live some of his favourite unique phrases, whether he's advising an MP to search for ‘a soothing medicament' or accusing others of ‘chuntering from a sedentary position'(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption was until recently a justice of the UK's Supreme Court, as well as being a distinguished historian. He argues that the law is taking over the space once occupied by politics. Presented and chaired by Anita Anand(n)

programme.desc: Justice of the Supreme Court Jonathan Sumption explores how democratic processes have the power to accommodate opposition opinions and interests, but argues that in recent years politics has shied away from legislating and now the courts have taken on more and more of the role of making law(n)
